description: Three of us noted the swift simultaniously as it passed very close to us and continued to do so for the next several minutes. Tyler and Brenda both had cameras and over 200 photos were taken over the next several minutes. The swift was small with quick wing beats. Size was judged by the nearby swallows which were also at close range. Swift was grayish overall with off white breast, white throat and light brown rump. Bill appeared to be quite small. From photos wings looked short but tail long. No calls were heard. Records indicate, that if accepted, this would be the fifth record for Colorado and the 2nd record for Montezuma County. Brenda Wright, Tyler Lausten, and Coen Dexter were searching through the very large flock of swallows when the swift suddenly appeared about three feet above the ground and at times within 10 feet or nearer to us. The swift remained in the area for the next several minutes and kept returing to our position near waters edge.
